name: Guan Xing (Anguo)
page_title: Guan Xing (Anguo) 關興 (安國) [Shu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Guan Xing was the son of Guan Yu. Unfortunately, there is not much known about him historically. He had several official appointments within Shu-Han and was said to be admired by Zhuge Liang, however (de Crespigny 276).

According to the Old Book of Tang, his descendants moved to Xindu.

In the Romance of the Three Kingdoms novel, he kills Pan Zhang and participates in Zhuge Liang’s Northern Campaigns alongside Zhang Bao. These things did not happen historically.

Work Cited:

De Crespigny, Rafe. A Biographical Dictionary of Later Han to the Three Kingdoms: (AD 23–220). Leiden [u.a.: Brill, 2007. Print.

full_text:
Guan Xing (Anguo) 關興 (安國)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Guan Xing (關興), styledAnguo (安國), was born in Xie County, He Dong Prefecture (Presently Yun Cheng in Shan Xi Province). The son of Guan Yu, he was greatly valued by Zhuge Liang. Guan Xing was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom. Guan Xing lived during the Later Han Dynasty period of Chinese history.
